message:
fix(doctor): VACUUM after light-repair leaves WARN-level orphan pages (#253)

`br doctor --repair` rebuilt stale `blocked_issues_cache` correctly
but left orphaned pages behind that surfaced as WARN-level
`page N: never used` integrity findings. These didn't flip the DB
into ERROR, so the pre-repair `report_has_page_corruption` gate
(ERROR-only) missed them — and subsequent `--repair` runs reported
"nothing to repair" while `PRAGMA integrity_check` was still dirty.

Add `report_has_warn_level_page_anomaly` (WARN-level, matches
"page N: never used" / "free space corruption" / "malformed" /
"disk image") and call VACUUM one additional time after the initial
light-repair pass if the post-repair report still has WARN-level
page residue. Guarded by `!local_repair.vacuumed` so we never loop.

This keeps the ERROR-only pre-repair gate unchanged (benign WARNs in
a fresh DB shouldn't trigger a VACUUM) while closing the gap where
light repair exposes or generates orphan pages that only VACUUM can
compact away.

Regression coverage in `cli::commands::doctor::tests`:
- matches_orphan_page_warn (all four message shapes from #253 report)
- ignores_non_page_warns (partial-index / out-of-order)
- ignores_error_level_findings (handled by existing VACUUM path)
- ignores_non_integrity_checks (sidecar warns etc.)

Closes #253 (external report from @cderv).
